mainEntity.name: I've been cleared for work but I don't feel safe going back yet—what are my rights?

acceptedAnswer.text: You have the right to a second opinion and a Functional Capacity Evaluation (FCE) to prove you are not yet fit for duty. Never simply 'no-show' at work; use medical evidence to contest the release and protect your benefits.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If you are still in pain or feel unsafe, you have the right to challenge your doctor's release. In Utah and Idaho, you can seek a second opinion from another qualified physician. You should also request a Functional Capacity Evaluation (FCE), which is a 4-6 hour physical test that proves exactly what your body can handle through objective measurement (not just a doctor's 'guess').

Do not simply refuse to go back; that is 'job abandonment.' Instead, go to work and document every task you cannot do.

- An FCE provides 'hard data' that is difficult for insurance to ignore.
- You can file an appeal with the Labor Commission if you were released prematurely.
- Your 'subjective' pain is valid, but needs 'objective' medical support.

A lawyer can help you find a doctor who specializes in complex work injuries and is not 'beholden' to insurance companies, ensuring your return-to-work date is actually safe.
